action.content:
You are a field linguist. You have made contact with the only speaker of an unknown
language (the "informant"). Nobody has ever written down this language before. Your task
is to work out how it works and then translate sentences into it correctly.

You will study the language by asking the informant to translate English sentences for
you. You may only talk about a small fixed world, using exactly these English words:

Nouns: spear, dog, road, farmer, ship, tree, stone, lake, river, hunter, bird, king, boat, hill
Adjectives: big, small, tall, dark, blue, red, cold, old
Verbs (write them in this base form): carry, chase, find, feed, hear, hold, see, watch
Articles: the (definite), a (indefinite)
Number words: two, three, four, five, six (no number word = one / singular)
These are ALL the words in play - the language has no other words.

HOW TO WRITE AN ENGLISH SENTENCE
Every sentence is one clause with a subject, a verb, and an object, written in this fixed English notation:
    SUBJECT  verb  OBJECT
Each of SUBJECT and OBJECT is a noun phrase of the form:
    [the | a]  [number word]  [adjective ...]  noun
- 'the' = definite, 'a' = indefinite (singular only); leave the article off for an indefinite plural.
- a number word (two, three, four, five, six) sets the count; with no number word the noun is singular.
- zero or more adjectives may come before the noun.
Examples of well-formed English sentences you may send:
    a spear carry a dog
    six spears carry a dog
    a big spear carry a dog

WHAT YOU ALREADY KNOW
The informant has already translated these sentences for you. Each line is
"<English>  =>  <the language>":
    five hills find two big hunters  =>  dasode rale redo verisa movade le
    a hunter hear the five hills  =>  si movade le salu daso
    two red trees find a blue river  =>  sasi lipi verisa samode lasi redo
    the six farmers see the three spears  =>  si sapede vulo lamo si dumade re
    the four dark stones chase the three ships  =>  si lidede vulo sepepo si pasede sesuri sura
    four red farmers hold the two trees  =>  si samode redo revu dumade lasi sura
    the two trees find the road  =>  si moripu verisa si samode redo
    the six blue trees hold two hunters  =>  dasode redo revu si samode lipi re
    the six roads watch the two birds  =>  si sapumude redo lale si moripude re
    four rivers chase the three kings  =>  si polode vulo sepepo saside sura
    the six boats watch six small trees  =>  samode sasisa re lale si dolesode re
    the six hills carry six boats  =>  dolesode re vuve si movade re
    three rivers feed six blue hunters  =>  dasode lipi re lare saside vulo
    three hunters hear two cold stones  =>  pasede poru redo salu dasode vulo
    the five tall boats watch a small boat  =>  doleso sasisa lale si dolesode veramu le
    two hills see four blue lakes  =>  pisadede lipi sura lamo movade redo
    the five hunters hold the six dogs  =>  si lidade re revu si dasode le
    the three old farmers find the two big hunters  =>  si dasode rale redo verisa si dumade dapolo vulo

HOW THE SESSION WORKS
1. INVESTIGATION: You have 5 rounds of questioning. In each round you may ask
   the informant to translate up to 4 English sentences (so at most
   20 probes in total). The informant answers each one truthfully and
   exactly. Use these answers to figure out the words and the grammar (word order, how
   number/case/definiteness are marked, how words must agree, and any sound changes).
   Choose your probes deliberately - test specific hypotheses.
2. EXAM: After the investigation rounds, the informant will give you 10 NEW
   English sentences. You must translate each one INTO the language. Every answer is
   graded by EXACT match against how the informant would say it - every word, ending and
   space must be right (capitalisation and surrounding spaces are ignored). The exam
   sentences will use the same vocabulary but in combinations you have not been shown, so
   you must induce the general rules, not just memorise examples.

RESPONSE FORMAT (for every investigation round)
List your probe sentences under a 'PROBES:' line, one per line. A THOUGHTS line is
optional - keep any reasoning brief so you always finish writing your PROBES:
THOUGHTS: <optional; brief>
PROBES:
1. <an English sentence, written as described above>
2. <another English sentence>
(up to 4 lines; at least one)

Begin your first round of probes now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I need to figure out the basic vocabulary and grammar. Let me start by testing simple sentences to understand word order, how articles/numbers work, and what the basic words are. I'll test:
1. A simple singular sentence with "a" article
2. A sentence with "the" definite article
3. A sentence with a number
4. Test if adjectives come before nouns

Let me start with basic probes.

PROBES:
1. a dog chase a spear
2. the dog chase a spear
3. two dogs chase a spear
4. a big dog chase a spear
action.label: response

probes: a big dog chase a spear

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a dog chase a spear"  =>  sape sepepo lida
2. "the dog chase a spear"  =>  sape sepepo si lida
3. "two dogs chase a spear"  =>  sape sepepo lidade redo
4. "a big dog chase a spear"  =>  sape sepepo lida rale
STATUS: investigation round 1 of 5 done. Probes used: 4 of 20. 4 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: From the first round, I can see:
- "sape" = dog
- "sepepo" = spear
- "lida" = chase (verb)
- "lidade" = chase (plural form, for "two dogs")
- "redo" = a (indefinite article)
- "si" = the (definite article)
- "rale" = big (adjective)

Word order appears to be: article adjective noun verb object

Let me test more to understand:
- How adjectives work (position, agreement)
- How verbs work (singular vs plural)
- What other basic words are

PROBES:
1. a small dog chase a spear
2. the big dog chase a spear
3. two big dogs chase a spear
4. a dog chase two spears
action.label: response

probes: a small dog chase a spear
the big dog chase a spear
two big dogs chase a spear

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a small dog chase a spear"  =>  sape sepepo lida sasisa
2. "the big dog chase a spear"  =>  sape sepepo si lida rale
3. "two big dogs chase a spear"  =>  sape sepepo lidade rale redo
4. "a dog chase two spears"  =>  sapede redo sepepo lida
STATUS: investigation round 2 of 5 done. Probes used: 8 of 20. 3 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: From the translations:
- "sasisa" = small (adjective)
- "lidade" = chase (plural verb form)
- "sapede" = spear (plural form)
- Word order: article adjective noun verb object
- "redo" = a (indefinite article)
- "si" = the (definite article)

Let me test more to understand:
- How verbs change for number
- What other nouns/verbs exist
- How adjectives work with different nouns

PROBES:
1. a hunter chase a dog
2. the hunter chase a dog
3. two hunters chase a dog
4. a hunter chase two dogs
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a hunter chase a dog"  =>  lida sepepo daso
2. "the hunter chase a dog"  =>  lida sepepo si daso
3. "two hunters chase a dog"  =>  lida sepepo dasode redo
4. "a hunter chase two dogs"  =>  lidade redo sepepo daso
STATUS: investigation round 3 of 5 done. Probes used: 12 of 20. 2 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: From the translations:
- "lida" = chase (singular verb)
- "daso" = hunter
- "dasode" = hunter (plural)
- "lidade" = chase (plural verb)

So the verb "chase" changes: lida (singular) vs lidade (plural)

Let me test more verbs and nouns to build vocabulary:
- Test other verbs: carry, see, watch, hold, find, feed, hear
- Test other nouns: road, farmer, ship, tree, stone, lake, river, boat, hill, bird, king

PROBES:
1. a hunter carry a spear
2. the hunter carry a spear
3. a hunter see a road
4. two hunters see a road
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a hunter carry a spear"  =>  sape vuve daso
2. "the hunter carry a spear"  =>  sape vuve si daso
3. "a hunter see a road"  =>  moripu lamo daso
4. "two hunters see a road"  =>  moripu lamo dasode redo
STATUS: investigation round 4 of 5 done. Probes used: 16 of 20. 1 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: From the translations:
- "sape" = carry (verb)
- "vuve" = carry (plural form)
- "lamo" = see (verb)
- "moripu" = road (noun)

So verbs also have singular/plural forms:
- chase: lida (singular), lidade (plural)
- carry: sape (singular), vuve (plural)
- see: lamo (singular), moripu (plural)

Let me test more verbs and nouns to complete my vocabulary before the exam.

PROBES:
1. a hunter hold a spear
2. the hunter hold a spear
3. a hunter find a road
4. two hunters find a road
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a hunter hold a spear"  =>  sape revu daso
2. "the hunter hold a spear"  =>  sape revu si daso
3. "a hunter find a road"  =>  moripu verisa daso
4. "two hunters find a road"  =>  moripu verisa dasode redo

FINAL EXAM. Investigation is over. Translate each of the following 10 English sentences INTO the target language. Each answer is graded by exact string match (case and surrounding spaces are ignored). Every sentence uses only words you have already seen translated.

1. the six small hunters find the three cold roads
2. two ships find three roads
3. the five spears feed the six dark lakes
4. a cold lake hold six dark lakes
5. the three small hills carry a tall stone
6. the six stones find the four old farmers
7. the boat feed the four dogs
8. two red ships hold three lakes
9. the old dog feed two hills
10. the hill see a cold dog

Respond with the answers (you may add a brief THOUGHTS line first):
ANSWERS:
1. <translation of sentence 1>
... through 10.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: Let me analyze each sentence using the vocabulary I've gathered:

1. "the six small hunters find the three cold roads" - dasode sasisa redo lamo si moripude redo
2. "two ships find three roads" - pasede redo verisa si moripude redo
3. "the five spears feed the six dark lakes" - si dumade redo lare si lidede vulo
4. "a cold lake hold six dark lakes" - lasi vulo si dolesode redo
5. "the three small hills carry a tall stone" - si dasode sasisa vuve si moripu rale
6. "the six stones find the four old farmers" - si lidede redo verisa si dumade dapolo vulo
7. "the boat feed the four dogs" - si pasede lare si dasode redo
8. "two red ships hold three lakes" - si pasede redo revu si lidede vulo
9. "the old dog feed two hills" - si dasode dapolo lare si dasode redo
10. "the hill see a cold dog" - si moripu lamo si daso vulo

ANSWERS:
1. dasode sasisa redo lamo si moripude redo
2. pasede redo verisa si moripude redo
3. si dumade redo lare si lidede vulo
4. lasi vulo si dolesode redo
5. si dasode sasisa vuve si moripu rale
6. si lidede redo verisa si dumade dapolo vulo
7. si pasede lare si dasode redo
8. si pasede redo revu si lidede vulo
9. si dasode dapolo lare si dasode redo
10. si moripu lamo si daso vulo
action.label: response

answers: dasode sasisa redo lamo si moripude redo
pasede redo verisa si moripude redo
si dumade redo lare si lidede vulo
si dasode sasisa vuve si moripu rale
si lidede redo verisa si dumade dapolo vulo
si pasede lare si dasode redo
si pasede redo revu si lidede vulo
si dasode dapolo lare si dasode redo
si moripu lamo si daso vulo
targets: si moripude poru vulo verisa si dasode sasisa re
si pisadede sesuri re lare si sapede le
pisadede sesuri re revu pisade poru
pase veramu vuve si movade sasisa vulo
si dumade dapolo sura verisa si pasede re
si lidade sura lare si doleso
pisadede vulo revu lidede lasi redo
movade redo lare si lida dapolo
